calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Particular Transactions (Probate Code Sec. 3100, Et Seq.)
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory Wilcox for and with (petitioner) Philip Leong. Lin Leong (son) also present. Brent Kato, Guardian ad Litem for Joyce Leong.

The Court stated that this is a petition to authorize a transaction with respect to the community property, to take out and make separate to avoid reimbursement to Medi-Cal. Mr. Wilcox stated that the primary aim is to qualify for Medi-Cal. The Court stated that the home would be excluded and would not be subject of reimbursement as long as the husband is alive avoiding the right of reimbursement and asked Mr. Kato for his views. Mr. Kato stated that he has done this for many clients in a similar situation, that it is a reasonable estate planning step, that he has never been challenged by Medi-Cal and that he has attended conferences where a Medi-Cal representative has talked on this subject. Mr. Wilcox stated that the primary issue is to qualify Mrs. Leong for Medi-Cal, that it is true that the house will not be available for reimbursement as long as Mr. Leong lives, that it is common to transfer the home, cited the Hart case, that it is a gift transfer and is what a reasonable person would do. Mr. Kato stated that another benefit is that if he sells the house he will be able to retain the proceeds but if it is co-owned it could cause Mrs. Leong to lose her benefits. The Court stated that Mrs. Leong is not in a position to make this judgment, that the evidence is persuasive and that it is ultimately for the benefit of her estate. The Court granted the petition for order authorizing particular transactions (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
